informal: Suppose $a \in R^{1}, f$ is a twice-differentiable real function on $(a, \infty)$, and $M_{0}, M_{1}, M_{2}$ are the least upper bounds of $|f(x)|,\left|f^{\prime}(x)\right|,\left|f^{\prime \prime}(x)\right|$, respectively, on $(a, \infty)$. Prove that $M_{1}^{2} \leq 4 M_{0} M_{2} .$
lean:
theorem Rudin_exercise_5_15_corrected
    {f : ℝ → ℝ} {a M0 M1 M2 : ℝ}
    (hf' : DifferentiableOn ℝ f (Set.Ioi a))
    (hf'' : DifferentiableOn ℝ (deriv f) (Set.Ioi a))
    (hbdd0 : BddAbove {(|f x|) | x ∈ (Set.Ioi a)})
    (hbdd1 : BddAbove {(|deriv f x|) | x ∈ (Set.Ioi a)})
    (hbdd2 : BddAbove {(|deriv (deriv f) x|) | x ∈ (Set.Ioi a)})
    (hM0 : M0 = sSup {(|f x|) | x ∈ (Set.Ioi a)})
    (hM1 : M1 = sSup {(|deriv f x|) | x ∈ (Set.Ioi a)})
    (hM2 : M2 = sSup {(|deriv (deriv f) x|) | x ∈ (Set.Ioi a)}) :
    (M1 ^ 2) ≤ 4 * M0 * M2
reasoning: The Lean signature captures all the relevant content of the informal statement: f is differentiable on (a, ∞) and its derivative is also differentiable there (encoding "twice-differentiable on (a, ∞)"); the sets {|f x| : x ∈ (a,∞)}, {|f' x| : x ∈ (a,∞)}, {|f'' x| : x ∈ (a,∞)} are assumed bounded above (so the sup is the actual least upper bound), and M0, M1, M2 are defined as those suprema. The conclusion M1^2 ≤ 4·M0·M2 matches exactly. The use of `deriv f` and `deriv (deriv f)` corresponds to f' and f'' under the given differentiability hypotheses. No extra hypotheses (continuity beyond what twice-differentiability already provides, finiteness, etc.) are silently added, and none are dropped.
